extraction.one_line_summary: ACKERMANN Pascal won a blanket sprint finish after Israel Start-Up Nation led out their own sprint train, taking his second consecutive stage victory.
tactical_patterns: VAN DER POEL Mathieu delivered a massive turn with 1km remaining before slotting back, exposing teammate abandonment issue.
Israel Start-Up Nation executed their own leadout for CIMOLAI into final 500m, yielding unexpected 1-3 finish for Germany.
ACKERMANN went too early into wind, sat back down, then re-accelerated—unconventional double-acceleration sprint won four-way blanket finish.
GAVIRIA finished second despite no visible UAE leadout support, revealing individual positioning strength compensating for team weakness.

rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: neutral: delivered exceptional 1km turn but teammates failed to materialize, suggesting Alpecin sprint coordination issues
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: won despite admitting only one training ride post-illness, first multi-leadout trust breakthrough after winless season
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: positive: second place without dedicated leadout reveals elite positioning skills, though losing 4s bonus time daily compounds GC deficit
rider_signals.CIMOLAI Davide: negative: Israel led out for him but finished sixth, indicating inability to convert team investment into results
rider_signals.BALLERINI Davide: neutral: came off wheel for glory but stalled, suggesting power limitation rather than positioning error in blanket sprint
